# Enjab UI

Version: 2026.06.05c

The Enjab Medical Centre design system, published as a shadcn registry. Hand an agent this file (https://ui.enjab.ae/llms.txt) and it can theme any project to match Enjab with no other input.

- Registry base: https://ui.enjab.ae/r/{name}.json
- Namespace: @enjab-ui
- Index of all items: https://ui.enjab.ae/r/registry.json
- Changelog and update prompt: https://ui.enjab.ae/changelog
- Live showcase: https://ui.enjab.ae (overview, components, dashboard, landing)

## Setup (pick one)

Direct, zero config:
```
npx shadcn@latest add https://ui.enjab.ae/r/theme.json
npx shadcn@latest add https://ui.enjab.ae/r/button.json
```

Namespaced (add to components.json, then use @enjab-ui/<name>):
```
"registries": { "@enjab-ui": "https://ui.enjab.ae/r/{name}.json" }
```
```
npx shadcn@latest add @enjab-ui/button
```

Install `theme` FIRST. It sets the Enjab design tokens that every component depends on.

## Updating an existing project

When Enjab UI changes, the changelog lists exactly what to do per change: https://ui.enjab.ae/changelog

A user updates a project by sending their agent this message:
```
Update this project to the latest Enjab UI. Read https://ui.enjab.ae/llms.txt and
https://ui.enjab.ae/changelog, then apply ONLY the changelog items this project has
not adopted yet. Re-install changed components with `npx shadcn add @enjab-ui/<name>`
(overwrite). Re-apply the theme if tokens changed. Do not refactor or restyle anything
else, keep the change surface minimal.
```

## Components

- theme : Enjab design tokens (colors, fonts, radius, sidebar, charts). Install first.
- button : pill-shaped button with a `navy` variant.
- status-pill : colored dot plus label. Status is never color alone.
- stat-card : KPI card with a mono value and trend delta.
- logo : Enjab logo. Light backgrounds only.
- reveal : Framer Motion scroll reveal. Landing pages only.
- enjab-byline : "an Enjab product" byline. Required on every dashboard.
- sidebar-footer : required sidebar bottom. Account block (avatar, email, sign out) plus the Enjab byline. Keeps all dashboards identical.

## Fonts (load these; the theme references them by name)

- Headings: Inter (Display optical size). Google Fonts, or next/font Inter.
- Body / UI: Satoshi. Fontshare: https://api.fontshare.com/v2/css?f[]=satoshi@400,500,700,900&display=swap
- Data (numbers, IDs, times): Fragment Mono. Google Fonts.

CSS variables exposed by the theme: --font-heading (Inter), --font-sans (Satoshi), --font-mono (Fragment Mono). Apply headings with the heading font, body with sans, data with mono.

## Rules (follow when building any Enjab interface)

- Light mode only. Never add a dark theme or dark: variants.
- Typography: headings use Inter Display, body/UI uses Satoshi, all numbers/IDs/times use Fragment Mono (the `font-data` class). Never put a heading in Satoshi.
- Color: teal #057C8B is primary, navy #1B3766 is depth. Always reference tokens (bg-teal, text-navy, bg-success, text-muted-foreground, etc.), never hardcode hex.
- Status colors (success #42AF48, warning #E0A100, danger #D64545, info #0099FF) mean status. Never color alone; pair with text or an icon.
- Logo on light backgrounds only. No reversed/white logo exists.
- Motion: animations belong on landing/marketing pages only, implemented with Framer Motion. Dashboards and internal tools stay still (instant color/state changes only) for usability.
- Spacing: 4px scale. Never let two sections, cards, or nav items touch. Default gap between blocks is 20px.
- App shell: sidebar brand header and topbar share the same height and border so the top reads as one connected bar.
- Every dashboard shows the "an Enjab product" byline: sidebar dashboards end the sidebar with sidebar-footer (account block + byline) pinned to the bottom; no-sidebar dashboards place enjab-byline anywhere sensible. Fixed sizes, never restyle.
- English, left to right.
- Stack: Next.js (App Router) + Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui. Charts: Recharts. Icons: Lucide.

## Audience

Enjab builds employee-facing tools (dashboards, internal consoles, automation), each in its own repo, often by different developers and agents. This registry keeps them visually identical.
